EXHIBITS IN SUPPORT OF PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS FILED BY PETITIONER HOLLOMAN MD, JOHN D.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER JOHN HOLLOMAN'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS CONTINUED FROM JUN-23-2008 TO JUL-15-2008. THE COURT REQUIRES THE MOVING PARTY TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PAPERS WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER JOHN HOLLOMAN'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE WRIT IS DENIED. PETITIONER HAS UNREASONABLY DELAYED IN SEEKING RELIEF. BUS. & PROF. CODE 2337. THE BOARD HAD JURISDICTION BECAUSE THE PROCEEDING WAS BASED ON THE MICHIGAN DECISION IN MAY OF 2003. THE DECISION IS SUPPORTED BY THE EVIDENCE AND THE PENALTY IS NOT EXCESSIVE GIVEN THE ASSESSMENT THAT WAS MADE OF PETITIONER'S ABILITIES IN THE MICHIGAN PROCEEDING. THE BOARD DID NOT VIOLATE PETITIONER'S DUE PROCESS RIGHTS FOR THERE WAS NO OBLIGATION TO RETRY THE MICHIGAN CASE. MAREK V. BOARD OF PODIATRIC MEDICINE (1993) 16 CAL APP. 4TH 1089, 1097-1098. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK MAHONEY; REPORTER: KAREN FARNSWORTH, CSR #5162
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUL-15-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
MOTION For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Jul-15-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER JOHN HOLLOMAN'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS

Appearances:
Neil S. Tardiff of Tardiff Law offices (805-544-8100) for petitioner and moving party
Jane Simon of the Office of the Attorney General (703-5544) for respondent and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the writ is denied. Petitioner has unreasonably delayed in seeking relief. Bus. & Prof. Code 2337. The Board had jurisdiction because the proceeding was based on the Michigan decision in May of 2003. The decision is supported by the evidence and the penalty is not excessive given the assessment that was made of petitioner's abilities in the Michigan proceeding. The Board did not violate petitioner's due process rights for there was no obligation to retry the Michigan case. Marek v. Board of Podiatric Medicine (1993) 16 Cal App. 4th 1089, 1097-1098. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR #5162

MOTION For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Jun-23-2008 Continued To Jul-15-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER JOHN HOLLOMAN'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS.
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED TO JULY 15, 2008. THE COURT REQUIRES THAT THE MOVING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE THE COURT WITH A SET OF COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PAPERS. WHEN SUBMITTING THE DUPLICATE COPIES, PLEASE PROVIDE A COVER LETTER TO THE COURT REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE SO THAT THE COURTESY COPIES WON'T BE MISPLACED WHEN RECEIVED. (302/PHA) JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
